title: Man convicted more than 3 decades after raping 2 women at gunpoint in Framingham store
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Stephen Paul Gale, 73, was convicted of raping two women at gunpoint in a Framingham store in 1989.
Gale evaded capture for over 30 years until Los Angeles police arrested him in 2024 after a DNA match.
Modern DNA technology and genetic genealogy linked Gale to the crime; sentencing is scheduled for March 30.

title: New Providence basketball coach goes viral for recruiting joke: ‘Just look at my wife’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bryan Hodgson introduced as Providence men's basketball head coach, tasked with rebuilding the struggling program.
Hodgson gained attention with his recruiting joke, referencing his wife, Jordan, during his first press conference.
Hodgson led South Florida to a 25-9 record and an NCAA tournament bid before joining Providence.

title: He’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon for children in foster care
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Alex Castrichini, 37, from Concord, Mass., will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for children in foster care.
Castrichini is inspired by his involvement with Boston CASA, which supports foster children with court-appointed advocates.
He aims to raise awareness of foster children's challenges and the importance of community support during the marathon.

title: ‘Children’s lives are at stake’: Boston teachers protest staffing cuts
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Over 400 Boston Public School jobs could be cut, affecting educators and support staff for high-needs students.
Boston Teachers Union demands $48 million investment to prevent layoffs; protests held outside City Hall before budget hearing.
Boston School Committee to vote on a proposed $1.7 billion budget, impacting schools with potential closures and reconfigurations.

title: Where ‘No Kings’ protests are happening around Massachusetts this Saturday
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Over 160 'No Kings' protests are scheduled across Massachusetts on March 28, part of a nationwide mobilization.
Massachusetts ranks second to California in the number of protests, with a total of more than 3,000 planned nationwide.
Protests address issues like Trump's immigration crackdown and concerns about democracy and U.S. involvement in Iran.

title: ESPN’s ‘Pardon The Interruption’ hosts believe Jaylen Brown should win NBA MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: ESPN's 'Pardon The Interruption' hosts support Jaylen Brown as the NBA MVP frontrunner.
Jaylen Brown averages 28.5 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.1 assists, leading the Celtics to second in the East.
Paul Pierce and ESPN hosts emphasize Brown's significant contribution despite Jayson Tatum's prolonged absence.

title: Marshfield police searching for missing 8-year-old boy
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Marshfield police seek public help to find Callahan Pierce, missing 8-year-old last seen near 236 Moraine St.
Pierce was wearing a black sweatshirt, black sweatpants, and carrying a backpack when he went missing Tuesday evening.
Residents with home cameras urged to check footage; avoid search area to prevent interference with police work.

title: Will James Hagens play for the Bruins this season? Don Sweeney responds.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens signed an AHL amateur tryout agreement to start his pro career with the Providence Bruins.
Boston GM Don Sweeney indicated the Bruins won't rush Hagens to the NHL during their playoff push.
Hagens impressed in college, but must prove his readiness in the AHL before potentially joining the Bruins.

title: Man convicted, sentenced to life in prison 40 years after teenager found dead in Plymouth forest
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Michael Hand, 69, was sentenced to life in prison for the 1986 murder of 15-year-old Tracy Gilpin in Plymouth.
Tracy Gilpin was reported missing October 2, 1986, and her body was found 20 days later in Myles Standish State Park.
Hand's 2018 interviews led to his arrest; he admitted to dropping a boulder on Gilpin, causing her death.

title: Pedestrian struck and killed on Mass. Pike
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A pedestrian was fatally struck by a vehicle while crossing I-90 East in Ludlow on Monday afternoon.
State Police responded at 12:30 p.m. and closed all travel lanes for 30 minutes for investigation.
The victim, whose identity is undisclosed, succumbed to their injuries at a local hospital.
